mechanism_narrative: A positive 3-month change in FEDFUNDS raises the discount rate applied to equity cash flows and tightens financial conditions, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if periods with rising 3-month FEDFUNDS produce positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of walk-forward triggered observations.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in UNRATE signals deteriorating labor demand and rising recession risk, causing investors to mark down earnings expectations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered UNRATE increases are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or if the trigger has negative precision below the unconditional negative-return rate.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating real activity in RSAFS indicates resilient household demand and supports near-term revenue expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month growth exceeds CPIAUCSL 3-month growth by at least 0.5 percentage points
failure_condition: Reject if this real-sales acceleration trigger has a walk-forward 20-day mean return below the unconditional 20-day mean return in at least two consecutive 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in INDPRO signals weakening goods-sector output and declining operating leverage for cyclical firms, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if INDPRO contraction triggers are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 65% of walk-forward observations.

mechanism_narrative: A rebound in HOUST while FEDFUNDS is falling signals improving interest-rate-sensitive demand and a positive impulse to construction-linked earnings, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST rises more than 8% over 3 months while FEDFUNDS falls over the same 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if the joint HOUST rebound and FEDFUNDS decline trigger does not improve 60-day positive-return frequency by at least 5 percentage points versus the unconditional sample frequency.

mechanism_narrative: Strong PAYEMS growth combined with a stable UNRATE identifies labor income expansion without rising unemployment stress, supporting consumption and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month average gain is positive and UNRATE changes by less than 0.1 percentage point over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations have a 20-day average S&P 500 return less than or equal to the unconditional average return over any 5-year walk-forward evaluation window.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated CPIAUCSL inflation growth when FEDFUNDS is already high signals that restrictive policy must persist, pressuring equity valuation multiples and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized growth exceeds 3.5% and FEDFUNDS is above 4.5%
failure_condition: Reject if the sticky-inflation restrictive-policy trigger is followed by positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 58% of triggered observations.

mechanism_narrative: Positive GDPC1 growth alongside rising INDPRO identifies broad real-economy momentum that supports aggregate earnings revisions, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 latest quarterly growth is positive and INDPRO 3-month percent change is positive
failure_condition: Reject if the joint GDPC1 and INDPRO growth signal has a 60-day forward hit rate below the unconditional positive-return frequency in two consecutive walk-forward test folds.
